revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:祭日]]
{{Otheruses|Religious ceremonies and significant rituals|other uses}} [[File:Omike(Yamatohime-no-miya) 01.JPG|thumb|250px|{{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} at {{ill|Kōtai Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}, at the annexed shrine {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Miya|lt=Yamatohime-no-Miya|lt=Yamatohime Shrine|ja|倭姫宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime Shrine|qq=}}, showing the ceremonial meal]] '''Festival days''' (祭日, Saijitsu) are days dedicated to significant {{ill|rituals|ja|儀礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|1=ritual|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q50726375}} in a religious context. In Japan, the term "festival day" is colloquially used to refer to {{ill|Public holidays in Japan|en|Public holidays in Japan|qid=Q1195354|lt=national holidays|1=Public holidays in Japan}}, although strictly speaking, they are not equivalent<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://dictionary.goo.ne.jp/leaf/jn2/85072/m0u/ |title=祭日（さいじつ）の意味 |work={{ill|Digital Daijisen|ja|デジタル大辞泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Digital Daijisen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大辞泉|1=Daijisen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5209153}} ({{ill|Shogakukan|en|Shogakukan|qid=Q785697|lt=Shogakukan|1=Shogakukan}}) |accessdate=2015-10-27 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web2 |author=Media Research Department, Hiroo Toyoshima |url=https://www.nhk.or.jp/bunken/summary/kotoba/gimon/165.html |url-status=live |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150131203141/nhk.or.jp/bunken/summary/kotoba/gimon/165.html |title=「祭日」「祝祭日」という言い方は? |work=Broadcast Terminology Q&A |publisher={{ill|NHK Broadcasting Culture Research Institute|ja|NHK放送文化研究所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/NHK Broadcasting Culture Research Institute|1=NHK Hōsō Bunka Kenkyūjo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11235313}} |date=2007-04-01 |accessdate=2015-12-09 |archivedate=2015-01-31 }}</ref>. This article focuses on festival days in Japan related to the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household|1=Imperial House of Japan}} ({{ill|Imperial Court|ja|宮中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|1=Kyūchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11453221}}) ceremonies and major religious traditions. == Imperial Household Ceremonies == {{User:Immanuelle/Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace updated}} Festival days were defined under the {{ill|Imperial Household Rites Act|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance|qq=}} as major or minor festival days. Many major festival days were codified into law as {{ill|Holiday|en|Holiday|qid=Q1445650|lt=national holidays|1=Holiday}}, leading to the combined term {{ill|Shukusai|ja|祝祭日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shukusai|1=Public holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588597}} ("holidays and festival days"). However, the ordinance was abolished on May 2, 1947, and there are no legally defined "festival days" in Japan today. Some of these dates have been renamed and designated as national holidays. === Major Festival Days === The {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} personally officiates ceremonies on these days. '''Fixed dates:''' * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3) * {{ill|Kigensetsu-sai|ja|紀元節祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (February 11): Now celebrated as {{ill|National Foundation Day (Japan)|en|National Foundation Day (Japan)|qid=Q1059995|lt=National Foundation Day|1=National Foundation Day (Japan)}} * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jimmu Festival|qq=}} (April 3) * {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kanname-sai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} (October 17) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November 23–24): November 23 is now {{ill|Labor Thanksgiving Day|en|Labor Thanksgiving Day|qid=Q82405|lt=Labor Thanksgiving Day|1=Labor Thanksgiving Day}} '''Variable dates:''' * {{ill|Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōrei-sai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} and {{ill|Shinden-sai|lt=Shinden-sai|lt=Shrine Festival|lt=Spring Shinden-sai|ja|春季神殿祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (near the {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|en|Vernal Equinox Day|qid=Q1049141|lt=Spring Equinox|1=Vernal Equinox Day}}) * {{ill|Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōrei-sai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} and {{ill|Shinden-sai|lt=Shinden-sai|lt=Shrine Festival|lt=Autumn Shinden-sai|ja|秋季神殿祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (near the {{ill|Autumnal Equinox Day|en|Autumnal Equinox Day|qid=Q1186410|lt=Autumn Equinox|1=Autumnal Equinox Day}}) * {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|lt=Previous Emperor Festival|lt=Previous Emperor's Festival|lt=Sentei-sai|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor's Festival|qq=}}: Anniversary of the death of a former Emperor ** During the Meiji period: {{ill|Emperor Kōmei|en|Emperor Kōmei|qid=Q299179|lt=Emperor Kōmei|1=Emperor Kōmei}} (January 30) ** During the Taishō period: {{ill|Emperor Meiji|en|Emperor Meiji|qid=Q174231|lt=Emperor Meiji|1=Emperor Meiji}} (July 30) ** During the Shōwa period: {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|qid=Q157052|lt=Emperor Taishō|1=Emperor Taishō}} (December 25) === Minor Festival Days === On these days, the {{ill|Chōtenchō|ja|掌典長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtenchō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498167}} conducts rituals on behalf of the Emperor, who offers worship. '''Fixed dates:''' * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1) * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai|1=Kinensai}} (February 17) == Religious Observances == Festival days in various religions, such as Christianity, Judaism, and Islam, are listed below. These vary according to denomination and doctrine. === Christianity === * {{ill|All Saints' Day|en|All Saints' Day|qid=Q587|lt=All Saints' Day|1=All Saints' Day}} * {{ill|Feast of the Transfiguration|ja|御変容の祝日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feast of the Transfiguration|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 主イエスの変容|1=Transfiguration of Jesus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q201201}} === Judaism === * {{ill|Rosh Hashanah|en|Rosh Hashanah|qid=Q131028|lt=Rosh Hashanah|1=Rosh Hashanah}} * {{ill|Yom Kippur|ja|ヨーム・キップール|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yom Kippur|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヨム・キプル|1=Yom Kippur|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132994}} === Islam === * See {{ill|Islamic holidays|en|Islamic holidays|qid=Q6484291|lt=Islamic holidays|1=Islamic holidays}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|Holiday|en|Holiday|qid=Q1445650|lt=National holidays (Japan)|1=Holiday}} * {{ill|Rituals of the Imperial Household|ja|皇室の儀式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rituals of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ritual Ceremonies of the Imperial Family|qid=Q11581030}}
{{translated page|ja|祭日|version=98854366|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:祭日]]
